questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 50°. The external angle at vertex B is 120°. Find the measure of angle C.
questions.explanation: The external angle at B equals the sum of the two remote interior angles: 120° = 50° + C. Therefore C = 70°.

questions.question: A triangle has sides of length 5, 12, and 13. Is this a right triangle? If yes, identify which angle is 90°.
questions.answer: Yes, the angle opposite the side of length 13 is 90°
questions.explanation: Check if 5² + 12² = 13²: 25 + 144 = 169 = 13². Since the Pythagorean theorem holds, it is a right triangle. The right angle is opposite the hypotenuse (length 13).

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle, each base angle measures 68°. Find the measure of the vertex angle.
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. If the two base angles are each 68°, then the vertex angle = 180° − 68° − 68° = 44°.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. The transversal intersects the first line at point P, forming an angle of 65° with the line. What angle does the transversal form with the second parallel line on the same side of the transversal?
questions.explanation: When a transversal crosses parallel lines, co-interior (same-side interior) angles are supplementary. The angle on the same side of the transversal at the second line is 180° − 65° = 115°.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle B = 90°, side AB = 7, and side BC = 24. Find the length of the hypotenuse AC.
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean theorem: AC² = AB² + BC² = 7² + 24² = 49 + 576 = 625. Therefore AC = √625 = 25.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, the angles are in the ratio 2:3:4. Find the measure of the largest angle.
questions.explanation: Let the angles be 2x, 3x, and 4x. Their sum is 180°: 2x + 3x + 4x = 180°, so 9x = 180°, giving x = 20°. The largest angle is 4x = 4(20°) = 80°.
